title: luzula campestris subsp. pulchella
intro: Luzula campestris subsp. pulchella, also known as the alpine woodrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sedge family (Juncaceae). It is native to Europe, particularly in the mountainous regions, and is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Luzula campestris subsp. pulchella is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to provide relief from pain and reduce inflammation.
